text: Polarized training distribution (~77% zone 1, 3% zone 2, 20% zone 3) produces greater performance improvements than threshold-dominant distribution (~46% zone 1, 35% zone 2, 19% zone 3) in recreational runners over 10 weeks.
Polarized training improved 10K time by 5.0% compared to 3.6% for threshold-dominant training over a 10-week intervention, a difference of approximately 41 seconds.
Among runners who most closely adhered to their prescribed intensity distribution, polarized training showed a large effect size advantage of 1.29 Cohen's d units over threshold training.
Both polarized and threshold-dominant training produce significant 10K performance improvements in recreational runners over 10 weeks of structured training.

Keeping zone 2 (between ventilatory threshold and respiratory compensation threshold) volume below 5% of total training time characterizes an effective polarized training distribution for recreational runners.
A 10-week polarized training program reduced recreational runners' 10K times from an average of 39:18 to 37:19.
